replies.msg:
各位大英帝國做緊嘢／將會做嘢嘅巴絲打，歡迎入嚟呢到鳩噏扮下工

Q&A credit to 滑蛋豬仔飯

Q: 如果無BC又想係英國打工係咪好難?

A:
通常會比working visa一係大公司
D細公司仔都見過有批不過當然會少D

老老實實渣BNO/特區係就係難D不過都唔係無可能
都識得有人搞得掂個visa
以前有同學會係cv度寫明話要working visa
如果間公司都肯in你既話多數即係肯批
唔洗浪費大家時間
大小公司都有 試下無妨

再唔係學其他巴打講
申請左working holiday做住先都未嘗不可
當然之後整唔整到tier 2 working visa就唔敢講
不過起碼都有2年比你係度做下野儲下經驗先

如果做開比較專業行業既朋友其實拎左working holiday visa先，再轉tier 2既機會都幾樂觀，都識幾個朋友都係咁，好似都係做design / I.T.之類 (by 賽麗娜Serena)


Q: 想問下英國見工係咪同香港一樣都係上網報?

A:
如果你話搵工過程其實同香港差唔多
睇你咩工啦
普通黎講大公司既話都係cv cover letter -> online test -> phone in-> assessment centre ( 可以好多round) -> final in
細公司就少幾個步驟咁


Q: 想問下係咪bno 同特區都可以fg 一年tier5 先，唔洗特登要搵肯比tier2 既公司住

A:
你英國畢業定香港想過去？ 唔係英國畢業唔肯定當地申請有無唔同但BNO 隨時可以申請tier 5 重有兩年，特區要抽架，都係兩年。
當地申請可能短d , 我當初因爲公司未令到可以簽visa 既licence 就幫我申請住tier 5 先，得特區又過左抽籤所以係visa 公司幫我係英國申請，但咁就得一年.. 無深究點解。
但全部香港申請過去既都有兩年。 (by 花生哥)

Q: 下年打算去working holiday 會唔會好難搵工同搵地方住（想住反係zoom1,2) 聽人講就話最好係香港搵定先去係好定唔好 我就做開酒吧個邊會唔會香港人 真心求教

A:
英國bar tender既需求量其實好大 因為佢pub既數量基本上同香港7仔有得揮 甚至係正常既餐廳入面都會有間pub仔/bar比人淨飲野吹水
所以你有經驗根本唔洗驚 去到英國任何一個地方都一定搵到野做

講我自己經驗啦 我就做好typical既西餐廳既 入面全部鬼佬
因為要升supervisor 所以gm要求我識哂餐廳所有部份 包括floor hatch同bar
Bar嗰邊我完全係一張白紙 連用咖啡機都唔識 基本上淨係識開水喉倒果汁呢d白痴都識做既野
去到依家講緊一個在既時間 叫做忙起上黎可以打哂三邊 聽日冇人返比人編左去自己睇bar

我覺得做bar最緊要係識同客吹水 尤其係餐廳bar
好似我地咁 係住宅區 長期都有熟客日日黎打"等" 真係日日風雨不改黎報到
黎到冇野做咪同bar tender吹水囉 所以你一定要好吹得

其餘都冇乜野擔心 驚咪去indeed search下囉 一大炸請緊人 (by 小野屋杏)

Q: 英國黎緊暑假畢業，準備搵工 （宜家4月中係咪太遲)

A: 唔遲
雖然有啲係截左
但仲有好多
留學生有優勢 就係唔怕relocation
成個英國既工你都apply得
宜家真係開始send cv都唔會太遲既
好多大公司如果唔係grad scheme既話其實一路都請緊人
以前好多同學都係8/9月先有offer
有D直頭下年1月先有工開
所以唔洗擔心既肯踏出第一步搵工就ok (by 馬壯FN2)

For those of you who were born in Hong Kong and received British Citizenship via descent wishing to return to settle in the UK, there are a number of things you may want to know -

1) As a British Citizen returning to the UK to settle, you are entitle to National Health Service (NHS) as soon as you are in the UK.

2) You can get social welfare benefits in the UK once you meet the habitual residence test. This usually means that you have lived in the UK for three months or more.

https://www.citizensadvice.org.uk/benefits/coming-from-abroad-and-claiming-benefits-the-habitual-residence-test/british-and-irish-citizens-claiming-benefits/

3) You should get a National Insurance Number (NI number) as soon as you arrived in the UK.

https://www.gov.uk/apply-national-insurance-number

4) You can exchange your Hong Kong driving licence to an UK one without test if you passed your driving test in Hong Kong.

https://www.facebook.com/HongkongerinUK/posts/861886470578180:0

但我想做 MT / 政府工多D - if you are thinking of getting a job in the British Civil Service, I would suggest you to apply for your Tier 2 (General) visa using your British National (Overseas) passport (if you have that status).

BN(O) passport holders are treated as Commonwealth Citizens in the UK. You can apply for British Civil Service jobs once you get Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) (永久居留) in the UK. This will give you a year head start as HKSAR passport holders cannot apply for jobs in the British Civil Service until they become British Citizens.

Working Holiday - British National (Overseas) passport holders can apply at any time while HKSAR passport holders will need to get a Certificate of Sponsorship (COS) from the Hong Kong Labour Department before applying for an UK Working Holiday visa. The next round of COS will be available at the beginning of 2018.

For those who have both British National (Overseas) status and HKSAR passport, I would suggest them to apply for UK Tier 2 or 5 visas using their BN(O) passports due to the following -

1) BN(O) passport holders with long term UK residence visa (greater than 6 months) do not need to register with the local police where they lived while HKSAR passport holders, as Chinese nationals, are required to register with the local police in the UK.

2) BN(O) passport holders are treated as Commonwealth Citizens in the UK. This means that BN(O) passport holders with long term UK residence visa can register to vote in the UK.

3) For Tier 2 - if it is your plan to get British Citizenship at the end, use your BN(O) for your Tier 2 visa and Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) (永久居留). When you apply for British Citizenship, you can register to become a British Citizen using form B(OTA), which is a cheaper process (registration £1163 vs naturalisation £1282 - both 2017 prices) than naturalisation (歸化).

東網透視：憑一支單簧管　香港仔入選御林軍

以香港第一人身份成爲英國御林軍樂手，David憶述，源於幾年前一次聚餐時偶遇英國陸軍音樂總監，對方提起英國御林軍樂隊招募新隊員，他持有英國國民(海外)護照(BNO)身份也可參與招聘考試，故決定一試。軍樂隊考試分兩部分，體能為先，音樂第二。對於當年33歲的他來說，體能測試是極大的挑戰，要10分半鐘內跑完2.4公里，亦要做掌上壓及仰臥起坐，首輪面試因跑步不達標而失敗。但他並沒有放棄，返港苦操3、4個月後再接受挑戰，2013年過關斬將通過所有測試，成功取錄。

身為英皇室的御林軍，薪酬福利令人關注，David則不予透露，僅指薪金水平較香港警察低一些，除普通的醫療和交通福利外，最優厚是住房福利，他與同事在倫敦市中心合住一間800至900平方呎單位，月租只需30英鎊，僅相當於300港元，相信令不少香港青年人羨慕。他現長居英國，還有一年便可入籍，目標是繼續在軍隊攀升，成爲終身職業。

係英國讀左三年書 (Bachelor)，出年會去Oxbridge讀一年 (Master)。佢岩岩收到實習既graduate offer (即係出年番工)。請問佢要做野做幾多年先拎到永久居留同BC。Thanks !


You can get Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) (永久居留) after 5 years on a Tier 2 (General) visa if you satisfy the requirements set by the Home Office (one of which is your income in the UK).

Please note that ILR will be revoked by the Home Office if the holder left the UK for more than 2 years.

Use your BN(O) passport to apply for your Tier 2 visa if you are planning to get ILR and then British Citizenship. BN(O) passport holders can register to become a British Citizen using form B(OTA) which is a cheaper process than naturalisation.

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/application-to-register-as-a-british-citizen-form-bota

有冇巴絲係英國做緊civil engineering 行業?
嗰邊難唔難搵工?



First question is whether you need a visa to work in the UK?

If you only hold a HKSAR passport, you can only get a Tier 2 Work Permit or wait until next year to get a Certificate of Sponsorship from the Hong Kong Labour Department before getting a Tier 5 visa. (Unless you were given a COS during this summer)

If you are looking to get a Tier 2 visa and have British National (Overseas) status, I would suggest you to renew your BN(O) passport if not already done so and use your BN(O) passport for a Tier 2 visa.

真心一問呢個係咩test黎

Does she has British National (Overseas) status? It is cheaper to get British Citizenship via registration using form B(OTA) than naturalisation using form AN. You can only register if you have a form of British nationality like British National (Overseas).

Both registration and naturalisation cost is not likely going to come down. The next rise will potentially be at the beginning of the financial year of 2018/19 (round about the beginning of April 2018).

I was looking back on the photocopy of the B(OTA) form I sent away for my British Citizenship all these years ago the other day. The form was only 4 pages of A4 sheets long and I got my British Citizenship for only £150! (It must be said that was nearly 15 years ago!)

Don't wait - the longer you wait, the more difficult and expensive registration / naturalisation will be!

唉 唔撚想知呀

真心一問呢個係咩test黎
唔記得左你搞緊乜sor li








Registration using form B(OTA) - for British National (Overseas) passport holders only - £1163.

Naturalisation using form AN - for HKSAR passport holders and BN(O) holders who want to pay more - £1282.

https://www.gov.uk/government/uploads/system/uploads/attachment_data/file/607005/NationalityFeesLeaflet_2017.pdf

The above are 2017 prices and do not include the cost of getting Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR). You need to have ILR before applying for British Citizenship.

我想問如果issue 左個visa, 有冇話最遲幾時要到英國 - the main problem for you is that Tier 2 visa holders can only stay in the UK for a maximum of 5 years and 14 days. Once you got your Tier 2 visa, any days you wasted in Hong Kong can affect your chances of getting Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) (永久居留). You will need 5 years residence in the UK under a Tier 2 (General) visa in order to get ILR.

https://www.gov.uk/tier-2-general

If you are not interested in getting ILR, then you can wait until you get your double pay in 2018 before coming to the UK.

International student are charged higher tuition fees by the universities in the UK.

Have a look at the example below -

http://www.ncl.ac.uk/postgraduate/courses/degrees/applied-linguistics-research-ma/#fees&funding

If you want to do a MA degree in Apply Linguistics Research at the University of Newcastle, the 'Home' fees will be £6,000 while the international fee for the same degree will be £13,980.

As a British Citizen, you will only be entitle to 'Home' fee status if you lived in the UK for three continuous years before the start of the course.

You should use BN(O) passport to apply for your UK Tier 2 visa due to the following reasons -

1) BN(O) passport holders with Tier 2 visa do not need to register with the local police in the UK while HKSAR passport holders, as Chinese nationals, are required to register with the local police.

2) BN(O) passport holders who have leave to remain for more than 6 months are entitle to register to vote in all UK elections as Commonwealth citizen. Being on the electoral roll helps you to build up your own credit history in the UK. HKSAR passport holders are not entitle to vote in any UK elections.

3) Once you have Indefinite Leave to Remain (永久居留), you can apply for UK civil service jobs in the UK that is open to Commonwealth citizens. HKSAR passport holders can only apply for UK civil service jobs once he/she has British Citizenship.

4) BN(O) passport holders can register to become a British Citizen using form B(OTA) which is a cheap process than naturalisation (Registration £1,163 vs naturalisation £1,282 - both 2017 prices). HKSAR passport holder must naturalise in order to get British Citizenship.

https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/application-to-register-as-a-british-citizen-form-bota

There are a number of ways to qualify for home fee status.

You can qualify if you are an EU national and have been resident in the either the UK, EEA, Switzerland or overseas territories for at least three consecutive years immediately before the first day of the first academic year of your course.

If you are a national of Norway, Iceland, Lichtenstein or Switzerland, you may also be eligible for the home rate of tuition fee if you:

have been resident in the EEA or Switzerland or overseas territories for at least the three consecutive years immediately before the first day of the first academic year of your course and,
are resident in the UK and working in the UK (as a migrant worker) by this date and
you continue to work in the UK during your course.

STEM科畢業會唔會易d揾工？



What kind of STEM subject you are talking about?

Here are a number of STEM subjects in the UK shortage occupations list. Being on the shortage occupation list means that it is easier for companies to get work permits for these posts -

1) Physical Scientists
2) Civil engineers
3) Mechanical engineers
4) Electrical engineers
5) Electronics Engineers
6) Design and development engineers
7) Production and process engineers
8) Secondary education teachers in the subjects of maths, physics, science (where an element of physics will be taught)
9) Quality control and planning engineers

https://www.gov.uk/guidance/immigration-rules/immigration-rules-appendix-k-shortage-occupation-list

Have a look at the above link for details.

If you have BN(O) status and was offered a chance to get an UK work permit, you should use your BN(O) passport to apply for your UK work permit.

Also if you have BN(O) status, you can joined the British Army and gain British Citizenship via service in the British Army.

What kind of STEM subject you are talking about?

Here are a number of STEM subjects in the UK shortage occupations list. Being on the shortage occupation list means that it is easier for companies to get work permits for these posts -

1) Physical Scientists
2) Civil engineers
3) Mechanical engineers
4) Electrical engineers
5) Electronics Engineers
6) Design and development engineers
7) Production and process engineers
8) Secondary education teachers in the subjects of maths, physics, science (where an element of physics will be taught)
9) Quality control and planning engineers

https://www.gov.uk/guidance/immigration-rules/immigration-rules-appendix-k-shortage-occupation-list

Have a look at the above link for details.

If you have BN(O) status and was offered a chance to get an UK work permit, you should use your BN(O) passport to apply for your UK work permit.

Also if you have BN(O) status, you can joined the British Army and gain British Citizenship via service in the British Army.

What about psychology and molecular biology & biotechnology(double major)?


You can still get an UK work permit if your job is not in the Home Office's shortage occupation list. As far as I know, your employer will need to conduct a resident labour test before being allow to apply for a work permit.

International student are charged higher tuition fees by the universities in the UK.

Have a look at the example below -

http://www.ncl.ac.uk/postgraduate/courses/degrees/applied-linguistics-research-ma/#fees&funding

If you want to do a MA degree in Apply Linguistics Research at the University of Newcastle, the 'Home' fees will be £6,000 while the international fee for the same degree will be £13,980.

As a British Citizen, you will only be entitle to 'Home' fee status if you lived in the UK for three continuous years before the start of the course.
